rationale: Cyrus the Great conquered Babylon. Same land, new Persian (Iranian) ethnicity.
Persian conquest of Egypt (525 BC). Same land, Persian ethnicity.
Alexander's conquest replaced Persian rule across the same vast territories.
Ptolemy I was a Macedonian general who inherited Egypt. Same Macedonian ethnicity, same territory.
Rome annexed Egypt after Actium (30 BC). Same territory, Latin/Roman ethnicity replacing Hellenic.
Arab Muslim conquest took the Levant and Egypt from Byzantium. Same land, Semitic/Arab ethnicity.
Parthians (Iranian, but Arsacid dynasty from Khorasan) gradually replaced Seleucid rule. Same land, Iranian ethnicity restored.
After the Hittite collapse, Achaemenid Persia incorporated Anatolia. Same land, Persian ethnicity.
Ottoman conquest of Constantinople (1453). Claimed 'Kayser-i Rum'. Anatolian/Balkan land, Turkic replaced Hellenic.
Seljuks descended from the Oghuz Turks of the post-Uyghur steppe era. Same Turkic ethnic lineage, migrated into Iran.
Babur was a Timurid prince who lost Transoxiana and conquered India instead. Same Timurid/Turkic dynasty, new territory.
Yuan is the Chinese branch of the Mongol Empire. Same Mongolic ethnicity, expanded into Chinese territory.
Ilkhanate is the Persian branch of the Mongol Empire. Same Mongolic ethnicity in a new territory.
Timur conquered the Chagatai territories. Both Turkic-Mongol in the same Central Asian heartland.
Alexander and the Seleucids absorbed Phoenician territory. Same Levantine land, Macedonian ethnicity.
INVALID — Tang is Han Chinese in East Asia; Ottoman is Turkic in Anatolia. No shared territory or ethnicity.
INVALID — Roman Italy/Mediterranean vs. Mongol steppe. No connection.
INVALID — Han China and Sassanid Persia are completely separate civilizational spheres.
